goal: Locate official NVIDIA documentation (page, version, heading) describing numerical accuracy bounds of the PhysX contact solver, or confirm no such published bound exists.
thoughts: Never fabricate a NVIDIA doc URL or tolerance number. lookup_knowledge is the only way to cite a real page — if it returns nothing, say so plainly. check_physics_health surfaces the exact setting paths (PhysxSceneAPI:solverPositionIterationCount, etc.) Thomas needs for his traceability matrix row. Solver tolerance is a convergence parameter, not an accuracy guarantee.
code:
# One citation row. Either return a real URL+heading or return 'not published'.

# 1. Retrieve — do NOT paraphrase from memory
lookup_knowledge(query="PhysX 5 contact solver numerical accuracy tolerance convergence Isaac Sim documentation")

# 2. Pull the actual scene solver parameter names + values
check_physics_health(stage_path="<SCENE_USD>")

# What to tell Thomas (two valid outcomes — pick based on lookup_knowledge result):
#
# Outcome A — doc found:
#   Cite: <URL_FROM_KNOWLEDGE>, section <HEADING>, Isaac Sim version <X.Y>.
#   Note: the doc describes solver iteration + tolerance knobs, not an
#   accuracy bound vs ground-truth physics.
#
# Outcome B — no doc found:
#   "NVIDIA does not publish a numerical accuracy bound for the PhysX
#    contact solver. The documented parameters are:
#      - PhysxSceneAPI:solverPositionIterationCount
#      - PhysxSceneAPI:solverVelocityIterationCount
#      - PhysxSceneAPI:timeStepsPerSecond
#      - PhysxRigidBodyAPI:solverPositionIterationCount (per-body override)
#    These are convergence controls, not accuracy guarantees."
# Do NOT invent 1e-4 or any other tolerance number.
failure_modes: Fabricating an NVIDIA doc URL when lookup_knowledge returned nothing
Giving a made-up tolerance like '1e-4' because Thomas asked for a number
Conflating solver convergence tolerance with physical accuracy
Claiming 'all physics engines have the same accuracy' — factually false
